rulings.Case Title: KIERAN BUCKLEY et al VS. DAVID BARTENWERFER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-518896 WITH CASE CGC-09-489793
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 22, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF KIERAN BUCKLEY, VERONICA CAMPBELL MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-518896 WITH CASE CGC-09-489793. Granted. No opposition filed. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes with a trial date of August 27, 2012. Case 489793 is the lead case and all further filings will be in that case only. The August 1, 2012 case management conference in case 518896 is vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH BALDOZ et al VS. VANESSA BUMANGLAG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 22, 2012, Line 9. CROSS DEFENDANTS JUDITH BALDOZ AND CHARLIE BORBE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. OVERRULED. A quantum meruit or unjust enrichment cause of action does not lie because Cross-complainants alleged that they intended Cross-defendant's labor to serve as compensation for rent, meals, and other related amenities provided by Cross-complainants. Cross-complainants have not shown that this defect can be cured by amendment. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA FRASER VS. LAMBERT DEVELOPMENT LANSING LLC, et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 22, 2012, Line 14. CROSS DEFENDANTS CLAUDIA FRASER AND JULIAN FRASER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to all causes of action. The court takes judicial notice of the Certificate of Final Completion and Occupancy. Cross-complainant is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing the causes of action are not time barred and the alleged refusals occurred during construction of the building on 40-50 Lansing Street. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA KEBEDE VS. FRANCIS H. CHUNG DDS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 22, 2012, Line 20. DEFENDANTS FRANCIS CHUNG AND FRANCIS H. CHUNG, D.D.S., INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The second cause of action is certain and states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff may bring a claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress under the direct victim theory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
